sources.label: QuiverQuant - Vicor shares slide as CEO stock sale draws attention after sharp run-up (Jun 4, 2026)
StockTitan - Vicor lifts Q2 2026 revenue guidance to $142M on new OEM patent licensee (May 26, 2026)
StockTitan - Vicor CEO sells 20,000 shares under 10b5-1 plan (Jun 2, 2026)

verdicts.rankRationale: No new information since June 6 and a flat tape today (-0.39%); the ~16% pullback from the ~$326 high with the thesis intact modestly improves asymmetry, but heavy one-way insider selling (CEO ~$111M over six months, 165 insider sells / zero buys) and the +134% YTD rerating keep it correctly parked in the Crowded / rerated benchmark bucket at rank 50.
verdicts.bull: Q2 guidance was raised from $126M to $142M on product strength plus royalties from a new OEM licensee of Factorized Power / Vertical Power Delivery patents, validating the 48V point-of-load bottleneck thesis; the post-insider-sale flush resets the entry without any fundamental deterioration.
verdicts.neutral: The licensing win and guidance raise are already in the price after the May 26 +13.7% pop and Needham's $350 target; the stock now digests sideways while the market weighs royalty durability against a rich rerated multiple.
verdicts.bear: All-sale, no-buy insider behavior near the highs caps sentiment, and at ~$12B cap with 134% YTD gains any Q2 royalty timing slip or licensee dispute triggers a sharp de-rating; the alpha here is largely harvested.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 results in July confirming the $142M guide and disclosure of additional patent licensees / royalty run-rate.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued insider distribution plus crowding unwind after the 134% YTD rerating; royalty revenue proving lumpy or one-off.
